mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates eliminate discount rate pressure on equity valuations, forcing capital into risk assets and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC below 0.02 during periods when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 9.0 percent signals labor market stabilization, which increases consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain negative or flat in 70% of months where UNRATE crosses below 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, creating a compounding demand shock that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM moves in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounds above 95.0 index points during a post-shock recovery phase, confirming supply chain normalization and triggering rapid equity repricing over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 65% of instances where INDPRO crosses above 95.0 during the specified recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate income and corporate revenue forecasts, generating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS MoM changes against 20-day forward returns falls below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index acceleration above 213.0 triggers central bank tightening expectations, which raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show positive mean or insignificant predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 213.0.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between GDP growth and retail sales indicates weak consumer transmission, which suppresses earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS shows positive predictive power for 5-day returns, contradicting the divergence thesis.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production stagnation below 97.0 coincides with elevated unemployment, creating a stagflationary supply constraint that forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in 60% of observations where both supply stagnation and high unemployment conditions are met.
